title: Zhaochen He
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Zhaochen He
Snapshot: 2026-07-21

confidence.rationale: Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.

biography: "When I first learned what the word omniscient meant, I was like YES, I'm that much closer!" - Dimitri Martin I attended college at the University of Chicago where I earned dual degrees in economics and physics. My PhD in economics is from Boston College. I was born in Beijing, China.
education_entries: Ph D in Economics, Boston College
BA in Economics, University of Chicago
BA in Physics, University of Chicago
research_interests: I am broadly interested in labor and macroeconomics. My skill set includes the use of statistical methodology to analyze large data-sets, as well as computational modeling under a variety of contexts. I excel at organizing information and connecting ideas from multiple lines of research.
teaching_interests: I enjoying teaching principles and econometrics. In class, I try to emphasize economic methods which can be directly applied in my students lives.
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2025). A census of the green economy: measuring marijuana employment, businesses, and payroll in four states . Applied Economics / Taylor and Francis. Volume, 57. Issue, 10. Pages, 1056-1069.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2022). The Impact of National Culture on Covid-19 Pandemic Outcomes . International Journal of Social Economics, Emerald. Volume, 49. Issue, 3. Pages, 313-335.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2017). The Contractionary Effect of Bad Economic News . Journal of Money Credit and Banking. Volume, 49. Issue, 6. Pages, 1339–1384.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2017). Failing Cities and the Red Queen Phenomenon . Boston College Law Review. Volume, 58. Issue, 2. Pages, 600-638.
